description: Lifecycle registry for long-term color-language contracts, bounded compatibility layers, and generated outputs.

contracts.label: Scheme Layer
contracts.purpose: Define the public identity, vocabulary, and abstract grouping vocabulary for every scheme.
guardrails: Keep all platform fields out of the scheme layer.
Change this layer when the public story or grouping vocabulary changes.

contracts.label: Product / Distribution Layer
contracts.purpose: Bind a public distribution identity to schemes without letting scheme manifests own marketplace, preview, or channel metadata.
guardrails: Product files may describe channels and product identity, but they must not define palette values.
Keep preview and release copy here instead of pushing product packaging back into scheme manifests.

contracts.label: Color Language Core
contracts.purpose: Own the abstract color language itself: families, roles, environment, guidance, interface, interaction, feedback, and terminal semantics.
guardrails: Prefer sparse anchors plus derivation over flat per-variant result tables.
Keep semantic meaning and environment relationships here, not in platform adapters.

contracts.label: Variant System
contracts.purpose: Control climate behavior, polarity, inheritance, and scheme-specific intensity knobs without redefining semantic meaning.
guardrails: Use variant knobs when the grammar stays the same but climate intensity changes.

contracts.label: Platform Contracts
contracts.purpose: Map abstract roles, surfaces, guidance, interface, interaction, feedback, and terminal semantics into concrete platform fields.
guardrails: Platform contracts translate intent; they do not define palette values.
Add new terminals or hosts here only after the abstract layer is already expressive enough.

contracts.label: Bounded Compatibility
contracts.purpose: Declare the small set of host-specific exceptions that intentionally remain outside abstract tone roles.
guardrails: Every entry must explain why the abstraction cost is not worth it yet.
This layer must stay free of design hex values and reusable palette decisions.

contracts.label: Calibration
contracts.purpose: Apply bounded readability, separation, telemetry, and asset-generation calibration without becoming a design authority.
guardrails: Use this layer for bounded compensation, not palette authorship.
Any calibration drift must remain explainable in lineage and parity.

contracts.label: Migration Anchors
contracts.purpose: Preserve stable compatibility snapshots while design authority continues moving upward into scheme and framework layers.
guardrails: Treat these files as sync-managed anchors, not philosophical sources of truth.
Only touch them manually when platform compatibility work truly requires it.

contracts.label: Generated Artifacts
contracts.purpose: Ship and document the current resolved outputs without letting deliverables become hidden design authority.
guardrails: Generated files should change only when sources or generators change.
Never hand-tune these outputs instead of fixing the source layer.
